title: Scavengers Reign
notes: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

title: Sugar
notes: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

title: The Lazarus Project
notes: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

title: Bodies
notes: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

title: Counterpart
notes: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

title: Patriot
notes: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

title: Utopia
notes: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

title: Rubicon
notes: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

title: Bad Sisters
notes: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

title: Detectorists
notes: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
notes: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

title: Somebody Somewhere
notes: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.
